era: 1985-1992. Proto-Metal. E=7 -- organized deliberate heaviness, pre-commercial Seattle. C/Z Records underground.

theory_nodes.concept: Jack Endino dual role: Skin Yard guitarist THEN Sub Pop production architect. Practitioner understanding of heavy guitar preceded production career. The engineer of Bleach and TAD's God's Balls learned the sound by playing it.
theory_nodes.description: Endino's production philosophy emerged from being a heavy music practitioner first.

theory_nodes.concept: Deep Six (1986, C/Z) -- the single most important document of pre-commercial Seattle heavy music. Skin Yard + Green River + Melvins + U-Men + Malfunkshun + Soundgarden. All major Seattle strands present four years before Nevermind.
theory_nodes.description: Degrees 338 and 339 are consecutive Deep Six alumni.

theory_nodes.concept: Matt Cameron throughline: Skin Yard (338) → Soundgarden → Pearl Jam (1997). One drummer's complete arc through the Seattle story, from proto-metal underground to grunge peak to post-grunge legacy.
theory_nodes.description: The most consequential drummer throughline in the engine.

cross_references.relationship: E=9 to E=7. Heaviest Sub Pop band (commercial era) to proto-metal predecessor (C/Z underground). Summit energy begins descent.
E=7 continues. Both Deep Six alumni (1986). Degrees 338-339 are consecutive proto-metal/proto-grunge entries from the pre-commercial Seattle scene.
Ben McMillan's second engine entry. Skin Yard (underground proto-metal, E=7) to Candlebox (mainstream, multi-platinum). Complete singer arc documented.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Jack Endino in metal context, 400-year canopy in full roar. Energy drops from E=18 as summit approaches close.
